items.title: LABO: LLM-Accelerated Bayesian Optimization through Broad Exploration and Selective Experimentation
items.abstract: The high cost and data scarcity in scientific exploration have motivated the use of large language models (LLMs) as knowledge-driven components in Bayesian optimization (BO). However, existing approaches typically embed LLMs directly into the sampling or surrogate modeling pipeline, without fully leveraging their significantly lower evaluation cost compared to real-world experiments. To address this limitation, we propose LLM-Accelerated Bayesian Optimization (LABO), a framework that combines LLM predictions with experimental observations within a single BO loop. LABO employs a gating criterion to dynamically balance the reliance on LLM predictions versus actual experiments. By leveraging inexpensive LLM evaluations to broadly explore the search space and reserving costly real experiments only for regions with high uncertainty, LABO achieves more sample-efficient optimization. We provide a theoretical analysis with a cumulative regret bound that formalizes this efficiency gain. Empirical results across diverse scientific tasks demonstrate that LABO consistently outperforms existing methods under identical experimental budgets. Our results suggest that LABO offers a practical and theoretically grounded approach for integrating LLMs into scientific discovery workflows.
items.llm_evidence_en: LLM-accelerated Bayesian optimization for scientific discovery
items.llm_evidence_cn: LLM加速贝叶斯优化，用于科学发现

items.canonical_evidence: LLM加速贝叶斯优化，用于科学发现
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LABO, combining LLM predictions with experimental observations in Bayesian optimization to accelerate scientific discovery by leveraging cheap LLM evaluations.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 科学探索中实验成本高且数据稀少，现有方法将LLM直接嵌入采样管道。本文提出LABO框架，在贝叶斯优化循环中动态平衡LLM预测与真实实验，利用廉价LLM评估拓宽搜索。该方法在多个合成和真实科学任务上显著加速优化，展示了LLM作为低成本和先验知识来源在自动发现中的价值。
